meta.title: SFS drum pattern library v2 (global expansion)
meta.provenance: Original stylizations of traditional and genre-conventional rhythms; not transcriptions of specific recordings. Key-pattern placements cross-checked against the sources in global-drum-patterns-report.md; kind='timeline' marks cyclic key patterns, 'source' cites the verifying reference.

patterns.name: Motorik — main
patterns.notes: Dinger-beat stylization: forward kick lean, relentless 8th hats.

patterns.name: Motorik — fill
patterns.notes: The pulse never stops; snare builds under the hats into the downbeat.

patterns.name: Basic rock — lift
patterns.notes: Open hats replace closed; bell step 1 as crash proxy.

patterns.name: Basic rock — fill
patterns.notes: Half-bar groove, then snare-tom run down to the floor.

patterns.name: Funk 16ths — sparse
patterns.notes: Cross-stick on cp.

patterns.name: Funk 16ths — lift
patterns.notes: Clap doubles the backbeat, shaker 16ths on hi.

patterns.name: Motown four — sparse
patterns.notes: Tambourine on hi.

patterns.name: Motown four — main
patterns.notes: Snare on all four, heavier on 2 and 4.

patterns.name: Motown four — lift
patterns.notes: Tambourine moves to 16ths, claps join the backbeat.

patterns.name: Blues shuffle — main
patterns.notes: Hat plays 1-and-a shuffle (skips the middle triplet).

patterns.name: Blues shuffle — lift
patterns.notes: Shuffle moves to the ride; hat foot on quarters.

patterns.name: Blues shuffle — fill
patterns.notes: Full-bar triplet roll, crescendo into the downbeat.

patterns.name: Classic house — main
patterns.notes: Offbeat opens, claps on 2/4, shaker 16ths underneath.

patterns.name: Classic house — fill
patterns.notes: Snare build over an unbroken four-on-the-floor.

patterns.name: Driving techno — lift
patterns.notes: Ride 8ths on bell for the peak section.

patterns.name: Driving techno — fill
patterns.notes: Kick drops out under the snare rush — tension, not addition.

patterns.name: DnB two-step — sparse
patterns.notes: Half-time intro feel.

patterns.name: DnB two-step — main
patterns.notes: Two-step: kick 1 and the 'and' of 3; ghosts around the backbeat.

patterns.name: Boom bap — fill
patterns.notes: Snare stutter at the bar's tail rather than a run.

patterns.name: Trap halftime — main
patterns.notes: Snare only on beat 3 (halftime); ratchets are hat rolls.

patterns.name: Son montuno — sparse
patterns.notes: cp = claves (3-2 son); chh = maracas.

patterns.name: Son montuno — main
patterns.notes: Conga tumbao on hi: slap on 2, open tones on beat 4 (steps 12,14) per pedagogy sources; kick = bombo (and-of-2, 3-side) / ponche (beat 4).

patterns.name: Son montuno — lift
patterns.notes: Stylized mambo-section bell; maracas to 16ths.

patterns.name: Son montuno — fill
patterns.notes: Timbale-style roll into the downbeat. Clave never stops.

patterns.name: Samba — sparse
patterns.notes: kick = surdo answer on 2 and 4 with pickup; hi = ganzá. Swing approximates the samba 16th feel.

patterns.name: Samba — main
patterns.notes: snare = caixa; cp = stylized tamborim telecoteco.

patterns.name: Samba — lift
patterns.notes: lo = third-surdo cuts; bell = agogô cross-rhythm.

patterns.name: Samba — fill
patterns.notes: Caixa crescendo; ganzá never stops.

patterns.name: Bossa nova — sparse
patterns.notes: cp = rim, playing the bossa clave.

patterns.name: Bossa nova — lift
patterns.notes: Ride 8ths and soft shaker for the lift — bossa peaks are gentle.

patterns.name: Bossa nova — fill
patterns.notes: A soft brushed drag, not a rock run.

patterns.name: Dembow — main
patterns.notes: The dembow snare: 3-3-2 across each half-bar.

patterns.name: Bembé 12/8 — sparse
patterns.notes: Standard bell (X.X.XX.X.X.X); hi = shekere on the dotted-quarter pulse.

patterns.name: Bembé 12/8 — main
patterns.notes: Low drum on the pulse, conga tones answering off it.

patterns.name: Bembé 12/8 — fill
patterns.notes: Drum flourish under an unbroken bell.

patterns.name: Afrobeat — main
patterns.notes: Broken kick, ghosted snare, busy hats — stylized Afrobeat kit part.

patterns.name: Afrobeat — fill
patterns.notes: Tom conversation; bell keeps the topline.

patterns.name: Reggae one drop — main
patterns.notes: Everything lands on beat 3; beat 1 stays empty — that IS the one drop.

patterns.name: Reggae steppers — lift
patterns.notes: The lift is the steppers variant: four-on-the-floor kick.

patterns.name: Maqsum — sparse
patterns.notes: kick = dum, cp = tek. The maqsum skeleton: D T . T D . T .

patterns.name: Maqsum — main
patterns.notes: hi = ka finger ornaments between the skeleton strokes.

patterns.name: Baladi — lift
patterns.notes: Lift is the heavier baladi cousin (doubled dum); hi = riqq; bell = finger cymbals.

patterns.name: Maqsum — fill
patterns.notes: Darbuka roll crescendo.

patterns.name: Son clave (3-2)
patterns.source: Toussaint, African Ternary Rhythm Timelines / Geometry of Musical Rhythm

patterns.name: Rumba clave (3-2)
patterns.source: Toussaint, African Ternary Rhythm Timelines / Geometry of Musical Rhythm

patterns.name: Bossa clave
patterns.source: Toussaint, African Ternary Rhythm Timelines / Geometry of Musical Rhythm

patterns.name: Shiko bell
patterns.source: Toussaint, African Ternary Rhythm Timelines / Geometry of Musical Rhythm

patterns.name: Soukous bell
patterns.notes: Clustered strokes 9,10 give the characteristic limp.
patterns.source: Toussaint, African Ternary Rhythm Timelines / Geometry of Musical Rhythm

patterns.name: Gahu bell
patterns.source: Toussaint, African Ternary Rhythm Timelines / Geometry of Musical Rhythm

patterns.name: Tresillo
patterns.notes: Seed of dembow, baiao, malfuf, habanera.
patterns.source: Toussaint, African Ternary Rhythm Timelines / Geometry of Musical Rhythm

patterns.name: Cinquillo
patterns.notes: Danzon baqueteo cell; Haitian ti-bwa; klezmer bulgar.
patterns.source: Toussaint, African Ternary Rhythm Timelines / Geometry of Musical Rhythm

patterns.name: Habanera
patterns.notes: Tango/milonga cell; early jazz 'Spanish tinge'.
patterns.source: Toussaint, African Ternary Rhythm Timelines / Geometry of Musical Rhythm

patterns.name: Standard pattern (Bembe bell)
patterns.notes: Ewe/Yoruba; Cuban bembe. Rotation start disagreement Anlo-Ewe vs Yoruba (Agawu) - flag.
patterns.source: Toussaint, African Ternary Rhythm Timelines / Geometry of Musical Rhythm

patterns.name: Soli bell (Guinea)
patterns.source: Toussaint, African Ternary Rhythm Timelines / Geometry of Musical Rhythm

patterns.name: Tambu bell
patterns.source: Toussaint, African Ternary Rhythm Timelines / Geometry of Musical Rhythm

patterns.name: Bembe-2 bell
patterns.source: Toussaint, African Ternary Rhythm Timelines / Geometry of Musical Rhythm

patterns.name: Yoruba bell (konkolo)
patterns.notes: Used in Cuban columbia (Toussaint).
patterns.source: Toussaint, African Ternary Rhythm Timelines / Geometry of Musical Rhythm

patterns.name: Tonada bell (Ashanti adowa)
patterns.notes: Also Guinea Mandiani (Toussaint).
patterns.source: Toussaint, African Ternary Rhythm Timelines / Geometry of Musical Rhythm

patterns.name: Asaadua bell (Akan)
patterns.source: Toussaint, African Ternary Rhythm Timelines / Geometry of Musical Rhythm

patterns.name: Sorsonet bell (Guinea)
patterns.source: Toussaint, African Ternary Rhythm Timelines / Geometry of Musical Rhythm

patterns.name: Bemba clap (N. Zimbabwe)
patterns.notes: Hand-clap/axe-blade timeline.
patterns.source: Toussaint, African Ternary Rhythm Timelines / Geometry of Musical Rhythm

patterns.name: Ashanti bell
patterns.source: Toussaint, African Ternary Rhythm Timelines / Geometry of Musical Rhythm

patterns.name: Pajdushko (Bulgaria)
patterns.notes: Grouping 2+3; strokes on quick/slow group starts (tapan dum). Pulse up to ~520/min in fast dances.

patterns.name: Racenitsa (Bulgaria)
patterns.notes: Grouping 2+2+3; strokes on quick/slow group starts (tapan dum). Pulse up to ~520/min in fast dances.

patterns.name: Dajchovo (Bulgaria)
patterns.notes: Grouping 2+2+2+3; strokes on quick/slow group starts (tapan dum). Pulse up to ~520/min in fast dances.

patterns.name: Kopanica (Bulgaria)
patterns.notes: Grouping 2+2+3+2+2; strokes on quick/slow group starts (tapan dum). Pulse up to ~520/min in fast dances.

patterns.name: Krivo Sadovsko
patterns.notes: Grouping 2+2+2+3+2+2; strokes on quick/slow group starts (tapan dum). Pulse up to ~520/min in fast dances.

patterns.name: Bucimis (Bulgaria)
patterns.notes: Grouping 2+2+2+2+3+2+2; strokes on quick/slow group starts (tapan dum). Pulse up to ~520/min in fast dances.

patterns.name: Kalamatianos / Makedonsko
patterns.notes: Grouping 3+2+2; strokes on quick/slow group starts (tapan dum). Pulse up to ~520/min in fast dances.

patterns.name: Curcuna (Turkey)
patterns.notes: Grouping 3+2+2+3; strokes on quick/slow group starts (tapan dum). Pulse up to ~520/min in fast dances.

patterns.name: Tintal theka (16)
patterns.notes: Vibhags 4+4+4+4; tali at 0,4,12, khali at 8 (bass drops steps 8-11). Sam = step 0.

patterns.name: Keherwa theka (8)
patterns.notes: Dha Ge Na Ti | Na Ka Dhi Na - the film/folk workhorse.

patterns.name: Dadra theka (6)
patterns.notes: Dha Dhin Na | Dha Tun Na (3+3).

patterns.name: Rupak theka (7)
patterns.notes: 3+2+2; unique: cycle BEGINS with khali (no bass on beat 1).

patterns.name: Jhaptal theka (10)
patterns.notes: 2+3+2+3; khali at step 5.

patterns.name: Lancaran colotomy (Java)
patterns.notes: hi=ketuk, bell=kenong, lo=kempul (wela/rest at step 1), kick=gong ageng WITH kenong on final step. End-weighted: strong beat is the LAST step, not step 0.

patterns.name: Ketawang colotomy (Java)
patterns.notes: hi=kempyang, cp=ketuk, bell=kenong (7,15), lo=kempul (11; wela at 3), kick=gong (15).

patterns.name: Bo Diddley beat
patterns.notes: 3-2 son clave orchestrated on floor toms.

patterns.name: Flamenco 12-count compas
patterns.notes: Accents 12,3,6,8,10 encoded from count 12 as step 0 (bulerias convention). Count-start varies by school - variants-disagree.

patterns.name: Malfuf (Egypt)
patterns.notes: D..T..T. - tresillo-shaped.

patterns.name: Ayyub / Zar (Egypt)
patterns.notes: D..D T.D. rendering; sources disagree on tek placement - variants-disagree.

patterns.name: Masmoudi kabir (Egypt)
patterns.notes: Slow 8/4: D D..T...D..T T.T.; heavily ornamented in practice.

patterns.name: Cascara (3-2)
patterns.notes: Timbale-shell pattern against 3-2 clave; stylized - schools vary on two strokes.

patterns.name: Danzon baqueteo
patterns.notes: Cinquillo doubled across the cycle; also the Haitian kompa ti-bwa line.

patterns.name: Slip-jig timeline (Ireland)
patterns.notes: 9-pulse jig-family cell, IOI 2-1-2-1-3.

patterns.name: Cumbia - main
patterns.notes: cp = llamador backbeat; lo = tambora; hi = guacharaca gallop (x.xx per beat), swung.

patterns.name: Merengue - main
patterns.notes: lo = tambora (low + rim taps), snare = roll pickup into 1, hi = guira 16ths. Tempo verified 120-160.

patterns.name: Bachata - main
patterns.notes: hi = bongo martillo 8ths with the verified beat-4 accent (step 12); chh = guira.

patterns.name: Candombe - main
patterns.notes: cp = madera (clave on drum shell), hi = chico cell (rest ON the beat, strokes on 2nd-4th 16ths - verified), kick = piano (stylized), lo = repique figures. Microtiming: pushed/contracted, near-ternary lean (Jure & Rocamora).

patterns.name: Candombe - fill (repique call)
patterns.notes: The madera never stops.

patterns.name: Festejo - main
patterns.notes: Cajon-led Afro-Peruvian 12/8 (verified meter); placements stylized. cp = quijada rattle.

patterns.name: Baiao - main
patterns.notes: kick = zabumba on the doubled tresillo (0,3,8,11); chh = triangle open/closed 16ths.

patterns.name: Chacarera - main
patterns.notes: Bombo leguero sesquialtera: bar 1 in 6/8 (0,3), bar 2 in 3/4 (6,8,10). cp = rim clicks.

patterns.name: Bomba sica - main
patterns.notes: cp = cua sticks timeline, kick = buleador, hi = primo answers (stylized), chh = maraca.

patterns.name: Soca - main
patterns.notes: Power soca: four-floor kick, engine-room iron 16ths on hi, cowbell offbeats.

patterns.name: Ska - main
patterns.notes: Everything leans on the offbeat 8ths (2,6,10,14).

patterns.name: Dancehall - main
patterns.notes: Double-tresillo kick (Bam Bam / dembow skeleton - verified lineage); cp = rim.

patterns.name: Kompa - main
patterns.notes: cp = ti-bwa playing the cinquillo timeline (verified cell); lo = tanbou (stylized).

patterns.name: Gahu - main
patterns.notes: Bell verified (Toussaint 0,3,6,10,14); kick/sticks from Pocket Operations kit reduction.

patterns.name: Gahu - fill
patterns.notes: The bell never stops.

patterns.name: Highlife - main
patterns.notes: Son-shaped bell; guitar carries most syncopation.

patterns.name: Kuku - main
patterns.notes: Stylized ensemble reduction (kick=dununba, lo=sangban, hi=kenkeni, cp=djembe accompaniment). Full parts: WAP Pages box-notation book.

patterns.name: Soukous - main
patterns.notes: Bell verified (Toussaint 0,3,6,9,10).

patterns.name: Gnawa - main
patterns.notes: bell = qraqeb gallop; ceremonies accelerate continuously - map tempo CV.

patterns.name: Amapiano - main
patterns.notes: lo = log drum on tresillo-leaning hits (3,6,10,13); half-time feel over house tempo. Stylized.

patterns.name: Gqom - main
patterns.notes: Broken 3+3+2 kick, no four-floor; dark tom stabs. Stylized.

patterns.name: Saidi - main
patterns.notes: D T . D D . T . (verified skeleton, khafif.com): dums 0,6,8; teks 2,12; hi = ka ornaments.

patterns.name: Saidi - lift
patterns.notes: chh = riqq 16ths, bell = finger cymbals.

patterns.name: Karsilama - main
patterns.notes: 2+2+2+3: dums 0,4; teks 2,6,8. Roman havasi adds offbeat teks - variants-disagree.

patterns.name: Shesh-o-hasht - main
patterns.notes: Persian 6/8 pop/classical groove; kick = tombak bass strokes, hi = tek chatter. Stylized.

patterns.name: Racenitsa - main
patterns.notes: 2+2+3 quick-quick-slow (verified grouping); tapan dums on group starts, accent on the slow.

patterns.name: Jig - main
patterns.notes: Bodhran: low on dotted beats, tip 8ths in DUD lilt.

patterns.name: Jig - lift
patterns.notes: Hemiola tease in the second half.

patterns.name: Reel - main
patterns.notes: Bodhran continuous 8ths, accents on 0 and 8; light lilt.

patterns.name: Bulerias - main
patterns.notes: Palmas + cajon on the 12-count compas (accents 12,3,6,8,10 from count 12). Count-start varies by school.

patterns.name: Keherwa groove - main
patterns.notes: Dha Ge Na Ti Na Ka Dhi Na as a groove (verified theka); kick = baya/dhol bass.

patterns.name: Bhangra chaal - main
patterns.notes: Dhol chaal: bass on 0,6,8,14 (bossa-kick shape), dagga tak on 2 and 4. Stylized.

patterns.name: Taiko matsuri - main
patterns.notes: 'Don doko' cells: kick = odaiko don, lo = doko doubles, cp = ka rim, hi = shime ji. Stylized.

patterns.name: Gutgeori jangdan - main
patterns.notes: Janggu cycle (verified 12/8 compound meter; stroke placements stylized): kick = kung/deong low, cp = deok rim.

patterns.name: 'Ote'a - main
patterns.notes: To'ere log drums at 16th density with 3+3+2 accents over pahu bass. Stylized.

patterns.name: Second line - main
patterns.notes: Kick walks the 3-2 son clave (verified basis); swung, laid-back. Snare = press-roll figures.

patterns.name: Tresillo E(3,8) — main
patterns.notes: E(3,8) kick under a straight backbeat.

patterns.name: Tresillo E(3,8) — lift
patterns.notes: E(7,16) bell against the E(3,8) kick.

patterns.name: Cinquillo E(5,8) — main
patterns.notes: E(5,8) kick — the cinquillo cell.

patterns.name: Euclid E(5,16) — main
patterns.notes: E(5,16) kick — quasi-bossa spacing.

patterns.name: Keherwa — main
patterns.notes: Dha ge na ti · na ka dhi na, one stroke per 8th.

patterns.name: Keherwa — fill
patterns.notes: Tirakita-style run into sam.

patterns.name: Teental — main
patterns.notes: 16 matras, one per 16th; khali (matras 10-12) drops the bass.

patterns.name: Teental — fill
patterns.notes: Tihai-flavoured: the figure states three times into sam.

patterns.name: Lancaran — main
patterns.notes: Gong on 1, kenong on the beats, kempul between, balungan 8ths.

patterns.name: Lancaran — lift
patterns.notes: Peking-style 16th doubling on cp.

patterns.name: Kotekan — main
patterns.notes: Interlocking polos/sangsih; together they fill the 16ths.

patterns.name: Kotekan — fill
patterns.notes: Unison run — the interlock collapses into one line into the gong.

sets.name: Motorik
sets.notes: Default set. Krautrock pulse — the hats are the engine.

sets.name: Son montuno (3-2)
sets.notes: Join sections only on clave-cycle boundaries.

sets.name: Bembé (12/8)
sets.notes: The bell never stops across any section change.
